summary: The user required the whole model-status section to render green when it is live.
The user asked for training-readiness questions to be checked against the repo and converted into queue items where gaps remained.
The user required Linux/WSL bring-up to become step 1 in the loop prompt for GPU efficiency.
The user asked for a new scale-analysis page explaining fine-tune scaling, data integrity, and why training from scratch is not currently justified.
captured_user_directives: Make the full model-status section render green when live.
Analyze training-readiness questions against the repo and queue any real gaps.
Add Linux/WSL bring-up as step 1 in the prompt loop.
Add a new scale-analysis button/page with its own repo-backed metrics.
captured_assistant_responses: The model card and nested sections were given live operational styling.
Real training-readiness gaps were mapped into task_queue.json.
The prompt loop now brings up Linux/WSL first for GPU-backed work.
A new scale-analysis panel now renders repo-backed readiness metrics and a fine-tune-first recommendation.
